topic: How game companies use AI for complex tasks

entries.ai_role: goal-oriented action planning for enemy behavior
entries.takeaway: Use local planning for discrete action sets, not whole-world autonomy.
Pacing control is often a stronger AI role than raw tactical intelligence.

entries.pattern: assistive drafting with writers in the loop
entries.takeaway: Use gen-AI to accelerate labor-heavy writing substeps, not replace authored voice.

entries.ai_role: parallel frameworks for more realistic bots
entries.takeaway: Hybrid stacks are the normal shipping shape.

entries.ai_role: assistant and squadmates react to player voice commands
entries.takeaway: Generative NPC interaction ships safest in support roles with strong fences.
Use ML where classical methods get brittle or too expensive.

entries.ai_role: RL, imitation learning, and vision-based testing
entries.takeaway: QA is one of the most credible high-value AI lanes because it is measurable.

entries.ai_role: mesh generation, scene generation roadmap, in-experience creation
entries.takeaway: Platform AI often succeeds by compressing creator workload first.
The next step after generation is controlled functionality, not just prettier assets.

entries.ai_role: strategy discussion, item retrieval, revive support, voice interaction
entries.takeaway: AI teammate patterns are viable when they are gameplay-aware and role-constrained.

entries.ai_role: RL and imitation-learning agent workflows for games and simulations
entries.takeaway: Tooling adoption matters because it lowers the cost of trying narrow AI loops.

entries.ai_role: language, speech, and animation support for game characters
entries.takeaway: Vendor infrastructure changes what studios can ship without inventing the whole stack.

summary.historical_pattern: planners, directors, pathfinding, authored control loops
summary.current_pattern: hybrid systems using ML or gen-AI for one bounded painful task
summary.best_practice: bounded intelligence inside a governed loop
summary.scbe_implication: use lane ownership, checkpoints, ledgers, and hybrid rails instead of unbounded AI autonomy
